publishedAbstract: - The Linux kernel RBD driver has a hard limitation of 510 snapshots maximum per image. This is a kernel-level constraint that the `ceph-csi` driver must work within.- These limitations are specially relevant for Openshift Data Foundation clients that heavily rely on this feature. One example is Red Hat Openshift Virtualization. Creating snapshots and clones of images based on RBD storage classes is a common task.- This article explores the limits when creating snapshots, the variables involved, and the consequences of exceeding these limits.- Please note that the scope of this article is limited to the RBD driver. CephFS does not have the same kernel-level snapshot limitation as RBD.
publishedTitle: Red Hat Openshift Data Foundation: RBD snapshots limits.
